{"credits": [], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-7817", "cvss": {"score": 6.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:N"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 6.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:N"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 7.1, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:N/PR:L/UI:N/VC:H/VI:N/VA:N/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-552", "name": "Files or Directories Accessible to External Parties"}], "description": "Local file inclusion (LFI) and server-side request forgery (SSRF) vulnerabilities in pgAdmin 4 LLM API configuration endpoints.

User-supplied api_key_file and api_url preferences were passed to the LLM provider clients without validation. An authenticated user could read arbitrary server-side files by pointing api_key_file at any path readable by the pgAdmin process, or coerce pgAdmin into making requests to internal targets (e.g. cloud metadata services such as 169.254.169.254) by setting api_url, exploiting the chat path and model-list endpoints.

Fix restricts api_key_file to the user's private storage (server mode) or home directory (desktop mode), enforces a printable-ASCII key shape and a 1024-byte read cap, and gates api_url against a configurable allow-list (config.ALLOWED_LLM_API_URLS) at every entry point.

This issue affects pgAdmin 4: before 9.15.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00217, "percentile": 0.11931}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-p58c-q354-6c4f", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-05-18T14:51:08Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-p58c-q354-6c4f", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-p58c-q354-6c4f"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-7817"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-05-11T16:17:38Z", "published_at": "2026-05-11T18:31:44Z", "references": ["https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-7817", "https://github.com/pgadmin-org/pgadmin4/issues/9900", "https://github.com/pgadmin-org/pgadmin4/commit/24485fe96", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-p58c-q354-6c4f"], "repository_advisory_url": null, "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/pgadmin-org/pgadmin4", "summary": "pgAdmin 4 contains local file inclusion (LFI) and server-side request forgery (SSRF) vulnerabilities", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-05-18T14:51:10Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-p58c-q354-6c4f", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "9.15", "package": {"ecosystem": "pip", "name": "pgadmin4"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 9.15"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: pgAdmin 4 contains local file inclusion (LFI) and server-side request forgery (SSRF) vulnerabilities
